data.title: Why Is Immunic Stock Soaring Friday?
data.description:
Immunic Inc. (NASDAQ:IMUX) stock is surging on Friday with a robust session volume of 55.97 million compared to the average volume of 1.62 million, according to Benzinga Pro data.

The surge follows the announcement of an oversubscribed private placement aimed at raising up to $400 million to accelerate its transformation into a commercial-stage company.

Equity Raise

The company announced a private placement of 229.08 million pre-funded warrants at $0.873 per pre-funded warrant, for upfront gross proceeds of $200 million.

In addition, the company will issue warrants to purchase up to an aggregate of 229.076 million shares at an exercise price of $0.873 per share, for up to an additional $200 million in gross proceeds.

The funding is expected to support the completion of the Phase 3 ENSURE trial in relapsing multiple sclerosis, with topline data expected by the end of 2026.

Immunic plans to submit a New Drug Application (NDA) in the U.S. in mid-2027, with a targeted potential regulatory approval date in 2028.

Immunic is working towards initiation of a phase 3 clinical program in primary progressive multiple sclerosis, which is expected later this year and estimated to take approximately 3.5 to 4 years to complete.

Management Transition

In addition to the funding details, Immunic has made significant leadership changes, elevating Simona Skerjanec to interim Chairperson of the Board and initiating a search for a new CEO with commercial expertise.

Immunic’s Co-Founder and CEO, Daniel Vitt, plans to transition to a new senior executive role focused on strengthening the company’s scientific strategy.

Technical Analysis

Immunic’s stock is currently trading 63.2% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 50.5% above its 100-day SMA, indicating strong short-term momentum. Over the past 12 months, shares have increased by 14.46% and are positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows, suggesting a bullish trend.

The RSI is at 69.12, which is considered neutral territory, while the MACD is above its signal line, indicating bullish momentum. The combination of a neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating potential for continued upward movement.

  • Key Resistance: $1.50
  • Key Support: $1.00

Sector Performance

Immunic is outperforming its sector significantly, with a daily gain of 24.12% compared to the Healthcare sector’s 1.54% increase. This strong performance places Immunic as a leading player in a sector that has shown modest growth over the past 30 days, where the sector has only gained 0.32%.

The Healthcare sector is currently ranked second among all sectors, indicating a favorable environment for stocks within this space. Over the past 90 days, the sector has seen a 4.40% increase, suggesting a positive trend that could benefit Immunic as it transitions to a commercial-stage company.

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating. Recent analyst moves include:

  • D. Boral Capital: Buy (Lowers Target to $4)
  • HC Wainwright & Co.: Buy (Maintains Target to $8)
  • D. Boral Capital: Buy (Maintains Target to $8)

IMUX Price Action: Immunic shares were up 23.56% at $1.07 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by PeopleImages.com – Yuri A via Shutterstock

data.title: Zillow Stock Drops Nearly 20% This Week After Earnings Miss
data.description:
• Zillow stock is trading at depressed levels. What’s ahead for ZG stock?

Revenue Beats, EPS Misses

The drop followed fourth-quarter results in which revenue of about $654 million slightly topped Wall Street expectations, but adjusted earnings of 39 cents per share missed the consensus by a penny.

Management guided first-quarter revenue to a range of $700 million to $710 million, ahead of estimates near $690 million, signaling continued top-line growth even as investors refocus on margins and profitability.

Legal Costs And New Rivals Weigh On Valuation

Longer term, Zillow could benefit if legal spending normalizes and the housing market recovers, but near-term uncertainty appears to be capping the stock's valuation.

Technical Indicators Flash Oversold

The RSI is at 16.12, indicating that the stock is in oversold territory, which could suggest a potential reversal if buying interest emerges. Meanwhile, the MACD is below its signal line, reinforcing the bearish sentiment surrounding the stock.

The combination of oversold RSI and bearish MACD indicates mixed momentum, suggesting traders should remain cautious.

  • Key Resistance: $45.00
  • Key Support: $43.00

ZG Shares Edge Lower Friday

ZG Price Action: Zillow Gr shares were down 0.32% at $43.56 at the time of publication on Friday. Shares are down some 19.6% on a week-to-date basis. The stock is trading near its 52-week low of $43.22, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: CRISPR Therapeutics Spotlights Momentum For Lead Gene Therapy As Adoption Widens
data.description:
Casgevy Uptake

Casgevy, the company’s gene-editing therapy for sickle cell disease and transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia, generated $54 million in fourth-quarter revenue and $116 million for the full year.

A total of 64 patients received infusions in 2025, including 30 during the fourth quarter.

Globally, 147 patients initiated treatment through the first cell collection during the year. Patient initiations nearly tripled compared to 2024, reflecting growing momentum heading into 2026.

William Blair noted, “the increase to 147 first cell collections strengthens confidence that higher initiation volumes will translate into materially greater revenue in 2026.”

Pipeline Advancement

Beyond hematology, the company continues to advance its in vivo liver editing programs.

CTX310 remains in Phase 1b trials targeting lipid disorders, while CTX321, a next-generation Lp(a) program, is progressing through enabling studies with updates expected in the second half of 2026.

William Blair analyst Sami Corwin wrote, “We continue to see the company’s in vivo cardiovascular gene editing programs as meaningful value drivers…”

Its siRNA-based candidate CTX611, developed with Sirius Therapeutics, is currently in Phase 2 trials for patients undergoing knee replacement surgery and may have broader applications across thromboembolic diseases.

CRISPR Therapeutics ended 2025 with $1.98 billion in cash and marketable securities. R&D expenses rose to $83.5 million in the fourth quarter, while net loss widened to $130.6 million in the fourth quarter from $37.3 million a year earlier.

CRSP Price Action: CRISPR Therapeutics shares were up 8.81% at $53.24 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by T. Schneider via Shutterstock

data.title: Small Caps, Silver Rally As Inflation Cools Further: What's Moving Markets Friday?
data.description:
After Thursday's broad-based tech selloff, Wall Street staged a rebound as a softer-than-expected inflation print reinforced expectations for interest-rate cuts during the year.

The annual inflation rate slowed from 2.7% to 2.4% in January, undershooting forecasts of 2.5% and marking the lowest reading since May 2025.

Core inflation — which strips out food and energy — also cooled, easing from 2.7% to 2.5% year over year, the lowest level since March 2021.

Small caps led the advance. The Russell 2000, a benchmark for small-cap stocks, climbed nearly 1.8%, outperforming its large-cap peers.

In commodities, gold jumped 2% to trade above $5,000, fighting to end the week in positive territory. Silver surged almost 4%, topping $77 per ounce.

Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) also caught a bid, rallying 4.6% and positioning to snap a four-day losing streak.

Rivian Automotive Inc. (NASDAQ:RIVN) jumped 27% — marking the strongest session in the company's history — after reporting a narrower-than-expected quarterly loss.

data.title: F/m Investments' SEC-Cleared Dual-Share Treasury Strategy Goes Live — End Of ETF Vs Mutual Fund Drama?
data.description:
Fixed-income manager F/m Investments on Thursday rolled out a new structure allowing investors to access its flagship 3-month U.S. Treasury bill strategy through both ETF and mutual fund share classes housed in a single portfolio — a move that could reshape how cash-management strategies are packaged.

The firm's Treasury strategy is now available via the existing F/m US Treasury 3 Month Bill Fund ETF (NASDAQ:TBIL) as well as a mutual fund share class, giving advisors flexibility without requiring separate funds or track records.

First Live Dual-Share ETF Structure

According to the firm, the launch represents the first operational dual-share-class ETF framework implemented under a modern exemptive order from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).

Unlike traditional setups where ETFs and mutual funds run parallel but separate portfolios, the new model keeps a single pool of assets, one management team and identical performance history.

CEO Alexander Morris said the goal was to prevent the investment "wrapper" from dictating investor choice, particularly when clients want ETF liquidity but also mutual-fund accessibility in retirement platforms.

For financial advisors, the structure aims to simplify portfolio management. Clients investing through brokerage accounts can use the ETF, while retirement or platform-restricted investors can hold the mutual fund version — without tracking performance differences or reconciling separate holdings.

Operational Backing And Industry Implications

The fund operates under The RBB Fund, Inc. trust infrastructure, while administration and operational support came from U.S. Bank Global Fund Services.

Chief commercial officer Josh Jacobs noted that demand for innovative ETF structures continues to rise as issuers look to blend tax efficiency, liquidity and distribution flexibility.

Why It Matters For ETF Markets

If widely adopted, dual-share ETF structures could:

  • Narrow distinctions between ETFs and mutual funds
  • Simplify advisor portfolio construction
  • Accelerate ETF penetration in retirement and institutional channels
  • Reduce operational duplication for asset managers

In short, investors may eventually choose the strategy first and the vehicle second — a shift that could quietly redraw the competitive map between traditional funds and ETFs.

Image: Shutterstock
